Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are going to create a temporal profile for the given supply chain process The activities are SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to consider every pair of activities i j such that i and j are in the same trace eventually following each other For example for a trace SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS we consider SS OP SS RC SS QI QI CA DT AS

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We are to estimate the average time and standard deviation for each pair Since the prompt does not provide explicit numbers we must estimate based on typical supply chain operations

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets think about the typical time gaps

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS to OP This is usually a quick step as once the supplier is selected the order is placed Average maybe 1 day

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: OP to RC This is the order lead time For high tech electronics this might take days to weeks Lets assume average 10 days with a std dev of 2 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RC to QI Receiving components and then inspects This can be done within a day Maybe same day or next day Average 1 day std dev 0 point 2 days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: QI to CA If inspection passes assembly can start quickly But if failed there might be rework or wait But in temporal profile we look at the time from QI to CA for the same product So after inspection assembly might start in 1 day Average 1 day std dev 0 point 5 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: CA to PT Assembly then testing The time depends on the assembly complexity Lets say average 3 days with std dev 1 day

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: PT to PK After testing packaging This is quick average 1 day std dev 0 point 2 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: PK to WS Packaging done then stored in warehouse Usually immediate but might wait for space Lets say average 1 day std dev 0 point 2 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: WS to DT Storage to distribution This depends on shipping schedules Lets assume average 2 days with std dev 1 day

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: DT to AS This is tricky because after sales support might start after the product is shipped and delivered We assume that for the purpose of this exercise we are modeling the time between the distribution event and the first after sales support activity Lets assume average 1 week with a std dev of 2 weeks because some products might have support triggered immediately and others after a long time

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But note the problem says a representative subset We do not have to do every pair We can pick a subset that includes both adjacent and non adjacent pairs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets choose adjacent pairs SS OP OP RC RC QI QI CA CA PT PT PK PK WS WS DT DT AS

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Non adjacent pairs that are still in the same trace and might be separated by multiple steps SS RC SS CA SS DT OP CA RC DT

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That gives 14 pairs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS RC This is the time from SS to RC It includes SS OP and OP RC So the average would be the sum of the average of SS OP and OP RC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly the standard deviation would be the square root of the sum of squares if the two steps are independent

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS CA This includes SS OP OP RC RC QI QI CA Average 13 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS DT This is the entire cycle Steps SS OP OP RC RC QI QI CA CA PT PT PK PK WS WS DT Average 20 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: OP CA Steps OP RC RC QI QI CA Average 12 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: RC DT Steps RC QI QI CA CA PT PT PK PK WS WS DT Average 9 days

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: However note that the problem does not specify that the steps are independent In fact there might be correlations So we can choose to set the non adjacent pairs independently Since the problem asks for an estimate we will use the independent propagation for the standard deviation as an approximation and then round to a reasonable number

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We will create a dictionary with these pairs and values
